# Anatomical Language

## Your objectives for this lab are to:

- Define and demonstrate the anatomical position.
- Use the correct terminology to describe the planes of the body.
- Identify the planes of view in several different views of the body.
- Define and demonstrate the different anatomical positions.
- Define and demonstrate the directional terms used in anatomy.
- Define and apply the different anatomical regions.
- Locate the major cavities and describe several of the organs that lie within each.
- Identify the abdominopelvic cavities:
  - Four quadrants and their organs
  - Nine regions and their organs
- Identify the serous membranes within the body and their respective layers.

## Prelab Activity 1.1

### Directional terms

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Anterior (or ventral) | Toward the front of the body |
| Posterior (or dorsal) | Toward the back of the body |
| Superior (or cranial) | Toward the head; above |
| Inferior (or caudal) | Toward the feet; below |
| Lateral | Away from the midline |
| Medial | Toward the midline |
| Proximal | Closer to the point of attachment |
| Distal | Farther from the point of attachment |
| Superficial | Near the surface |
| Deep | Farther from the surface |

### Body Cavities

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Cranial | Contains the brain |
| Abdominal | Contains digestive organs |
| Pericardial | Contains the heart |
| Pleural | Contains the lungs |
| Ventral | Anterior cavity containing thoracic and abdominopelvic cavities |

### Body Planes

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Sagittal plane | Divides body into left and right |
| Midsagittal plane | Divides body into equal halves |
| Parasagittal plane | Divides body into unequal halves |
| Frontal plane | Divides body into anterior and posterior |
| Transverse plane | Divides body into superior and inferior |
| Oblique plane | Divides body at an angle |

## Prelab Activity 1.2

| Cavity number | Cavity or Structure |
|---|---|
| 1 | Cranial cavity |
| 2 | Vertebral (spinal) cavity |
| 3 | Thoracic cavity |
| 4 | Abdominal cavity |
| 5 | Pelvic cavity |
| 6 | Dorsal cavity |
| 7 | Ventral cavity |
| a | Pleural cavity |
| b | Pericardial cavity |
| c | Mediastinum |
| d | Diaphragm |
| e | Abdominopelvic cavity |

## Prelab Activity 1.3

| # | Organ System | Function | Organs |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Integumentary | Protection, temperature regulation | Skin, hair, nails |
| 2 | Skeletal | Support, protection | Bones |
| 3 | Muscular | Movement | Muscles |
| 4 | Nervous | Control and communication | Brain, nerves |
| 5 | Endocrine | Hormone secretion | Thyroid, pituitary |
| 6 | Cardiovascular | Transport | Heart, blood vessels |
| 7 | Lymphatic | Immunity | Lymph nodes |
| 8 | Respiratory | Gas exchange | Lungs |
| 9 | Digestive | Nutrient absorption | Stomach, intestines |
| 10 | Urinary | Waste removal | Kidneys |
| 11a | Reproductive (Male) | Sperm production | Testes |
| 11b | Reproductive (Female) | Egg production | Ovaries |

## Lab Activity 1.4

1. The elbow is located **proximal** to the wrist.  
2. The umbilicus is located **inferior** to the sternum.  
3. The nose is located **medial** to the ears.  
4. The mouth is located **superior** to the chin.  
5. A papercut is **superficial**; a nail puncture is **deep**.  
6. A wound on the elbow is **proximal** to the wrist.  

## Lab Activity 1.6

- Figure 1.10 plane: **Transverse**
- Figure 1.11 plane: **Sagittal**

## Lab Activity 1.8

### Abdominal Quadrants

| Quadrant | Organs |
|---|---|
| Right upper | Liver, gallbladder |
| Right lower | Appendix, cecum |
| Left upper | Stomach, spleen |
| Left lower | Sigmoid colon |

### Abdominal Regions

| Region | Organs |
|---|---|
| Left hypochondriac | Spleen |
| Epigastric | Stomach |
| Right hypochondriac | Liver |
| Left lumbar | Descending colon |
| Umbilical | Small intestine |
| Right lumbar | Ascending colon |
| Left iliac | Sigmoid colon |
| Hypogastric | Urinary bladder |
| Right iliac | Appendix |

## Post Lab Activity 1.1

### Systems

- Integumentary
- Integumentary
- Integumentary
- Endocrine
- Respiratory
- Reproductive

### Homeostasis

- Positive feedback
- Negative feedback

### Position terms

- Knees are **proximal** to the ankles  
- Thumbs are **lateral** to the pinky fingers  
- Spine is **posterior** to the breastbone  
- Chest is **anterior** to the shoulder blades  
- Pinky fingers are **medial** to the thumbs  
- Navel is **anterior** to the lower back  
- Eyes are **lateral** to the bridge of the nose  
- Breasts are **superficial** to the lungs  
- Intestines are **inferior** to the neck  
- Nose is **superior** to the mouth  
- Elbows are **proximal** to wrists  
- Mouth is **inferior** to the forehead  
- Calf is **posterior** to the shin  
- Heart is **medial** to the ribcage  
- Genitals are **medial** to the hips  
- Ankles are **distal** to the shins  
- Nipples are **superior** to the knees  
- Lips are **medial** and **anterior** to the ears  
- Brain is **deep** to the skull  
- Thighs are **proximal** to the feet  
- Lower back is **posterior** to the navel  
- Ribcage is **superficial** to the lungs  
- Skin is **superficial** to the muscles  

### Regional terms

- Nasal: nose  
- Oral: mouth  
- Cervical: neck  
- Acromial: shoulder  
- Axillary: armpit  
- Abdominal: belly  
- Brachial: arm  
- Antecubital: front of elbow  
- Antebrachial: forearm  
- Pelvic: pelvis  

### Planes

- Frontal (coronal)
- Transverse (horizontal)

### Cavities

- Dorsal
- Dorsal
- Ventral
- Ventral
- Cranial
- Vertebral

## Post Lab Activity 1.2

1. d  
2. b  
3. c  
4. b  
5. c  
6. d  
7. d  
8. d  
9. d  
10. c
